summary: Recent updates to the HTMLMediaElement in Firefox introduced important changes to how media playback behaves when content is muted. Specifically, the updates allow the muted content attribute to serve as a fallback for the muted state, meaning that if the media is supposed to be muted, the browser will respect this setting more consistently across different types of media elements. This enhancement improves how users experience audio and video playback without unexpected sounds, reinforcing user control over media playback. **This is particularly useful in scenarios where users may want to prevent sounds from playing automatically, contributing to a more user-friendly browsing environment.**
